Apple___Apple_scab.name: Apple Scab
symptoms: Olive-green to brown velvety spots on leaves and fruit
Premature leaf drop in severe cases
Dark, scabby lesions on fruit surface
organic_treatments: Apply sulfur-based fungicides every 7-10 days during wet weather
Use neem oil spray (2ml per liter water) as preventive measure
Remove and destroy fallen leaves in autumn
Apply compost tea to boost plant immunity
chemical_treatments: Captan 50 WP: Apply at 2g per liter water during green tip stage
Myclobutanil: 1.5ml per liter water, apply every 10-14 days
Fenbuconazole: Apply at first sign of disease
prevention: Prune trees annually for good air circulation
Clean up and destroy fallen leaves and fruit in autumn
Apply dormant oil spray in early spring before bud break

Apple___Black_rot.name: Black Rot
symptoms: Purple to red spots on leaves that enlarge to brown
Frogeye leaf spots with purple margins
Brown, firm rot on fruit that turns black
Mummified fruit that remains on tree
organic_treatments: Apply copper fungicides early in growing season
Remove and destroy infected fruit and branches
Prune out cankers during dormant season
Apply compost tea to strengthen tree immunity
chemical_treatments: Captan: 2g per liter water, apply every 10-14 days
prevention: Remove mummified fruit from tree and ground
Prune out infected branches 6-8 inches below canker
Maintain tree vigor with proper fertilization

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.name: Cedar Apple Rust
symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surface
Small black dots within orange spots
Tubular structures on underside of leaves
organic_treatments: Apply sulfur sprays at 7-10 day intervals during wet periods
Remove eastern red cedar trees within 1-2 miles if possible
Prune galls from cedar trees in late winter
Apply neem oil as preventive spray
chemical_treatments: Myclobutanil: 1.5ml per liter water, apply at pink bud stage
Propiconazole: Apply at first sign of infection
prevention: Remove juniper and cedar trees within 1-2 mile radius
Apply fungicides from pink bud through petal fall

Apple___healthy.name: Healthy Apple Tree
symptoms: Healthy, vibrant green leaves with no spots or discoloration
organic_treatments: Continue regular compost applications in spring and fall
Apply compost tea monthly during growing season
Maintain mulch layer around base to retain moisture
Use neem oil spray as preventive measure every 2 weeks
chemical_treatments: No chemical treatment needed for healthy plants
Apply dormant oil in early spring if desired
prevention: Water deeply at base of tree, avoid overhead irrigation
Apply balanced fertilizer in early spring
Monitor weekly for early signs of disease

Blueberry___healthy.name: Healthy Blueberry
organic_treatments: Apply composted pine bark mulch annually
Use fish emulsion fertilizer in spring
Apply compost tea monthly during growing season
Maintain acidic soil pH (4.5-5.2) with sulfur
chemical_treatments: Continue with acidifying fertilizer if needed
prevention: Mulch with pine bark or pine needles
Prune out old canes in late winter

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.name: Healthy Cherry
organic_treatments: Apply compost in early spring
Use fish emulsion fertilizer monthly
Mulch with organic material
Apply seaweed extract for stress resistance
chemical_treatments: Apply balanced fertilizer in early spring if needed
prevention: Prune in late summer to prevent silver leaf disease
Water at base, avoid wetting foliage

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.name: Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White, powdery fungal growth on leaves and shoots
organic_treatments: Apply baking soda spray (1 tbsp per gallon water + 1 tsp soap) weekly
Use neem oil (2ml per liter) every 7-10 days
Milk spray (1 part milk to 9 parts water) as preventive
Apply sulfur dust when temperatures are below 85°F
chemical_treatments: Myclobutanil: 1.5ml per liter water, apply at first sign
prevention: Ensure good air circulation through pruning
Avoid overhead watering, water at base
Remove and destroy infected plant debris

Corn_(maize)___healthy.name: Healthy Corn
organic_treatments: Apply compost or aged manure before planting
Use fish emulsion fertilizer at knee-high stage
Side-dress with compost tea during growth
Apply seaweed extract for micronutrients
chemical_treatments: Apply balanced fertilizer according to soil test
prevention: Irrigate at base, avoid overhead watering

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.name: Common Rust
symptoms: Small, oval reddish-brown pustules on leaves
Pustules turn dark brown to black as they mature
Yellowing of leaf tissue around pustules
organic_treatments: Apply sulfur dust at 7-10 day intervals
Use neem oil as preventive spray
Plant resistant varieties when available
Apply compost tea to boost immunity
chemical_treatments: Azoxystrobin: Apply at first sign of rust
Mancozeb: 2g per liter water for prevention
prevention: Plant resistant hybrids with rust resistance genes
Plant early to avoid peak rust periods
Maintain proper plant spacing for air circulation

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.name: Northern Leaf Blight
symptoms: Long, cigar-shaped gray-green lesions on leaves
Lesions turn tan to gray with dark borders
organic_treatments: Apply copper fungicides at early signs
Remove and destroy infected crop residue
Practice 2-3 year crop rotation
Apply compost tea to boost resistance
chemical_treatments: Azoxystrobin: Apply at first sign of lesions
Mancozeb: 2.5g per liter water for prevention
prevention: Plant resistant hybrids with Ht genes
Practice crop rotation with non-grass crops

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.name: Gray Leaf Spot
symptoms: Small, rectangular to oval gray spots on leaves
Lesions run parallel to leaf veins
organic_treatments: Apply copper fungicides at first sign
Rotate with non-host crops for 2-3 years
Remove and destroy infected crop residue
Use compost tea to strengthen plants

Grape___healthy.name: Healthy Grape
symptoms: Healthy green leaves with good texture
organic_treatments: Apply compost in spring
Use fish emulsion fertilizer monthly
Mulch with organic material
Apply seaweed extract for stress tolerance
chemical_treatments: Apply balanced fertilizer according to soil test
prevention: Maintain good air circulation through training
Water at base, avoid wetting foliage

Grape___Black_rot.name: Black Rot
symptoms: Small, circular brown spots on leaves
Spots develop dark borders and black centers
Dark cankers on stems and tendrils
organic_treatments: Apply copper fungicides at bud break
Sulfur sprays every 7-10 days during wet periods
Remove and destroy mummified berries
Prune infected canes during dormant season
chemical_treatments: Myclobutanil: Apply at 1-2 inch shoot growth
Captan: 2g per liter water, apply before bloom
prevention: Apply dormant sprays in early spring

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).name: Esca (Black Measles)
symptoms: Tiger-stripe patterns on leaves (dark green between veins)
Black spots and measles on fruit
White rot and decay in trunk and arms
Sudden wilting and death of shoots
organic_treatments: Remove infected vines completely
Use healthy, certified planting material
Apply Trichoderma beneficial fungi
Prune during dry weather only
chemical_treatments: No effective chemical cure - prevention only
Sodium arsenite: Restricted use, consult extension
Apply trunk injection of fungicides in severe cases
prevention: Avoid planting in areas with history of disease

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.name: Leaf Blight
organic_treatments: Apply copper sprays at 10-14 day intervals
Use sulfur applications during dry weather
Remove and destroy infected leaves
Apply compost tea to boost immunity
chemical_treatments: Mancozeb: 2g per liter water at first sign
prevention: Train vines on trellis for air flow

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).name: Citrus Greening (Huanglongbing)

organic_treatments: Remove and destroy infected trees immediately
Control Asian citrus psyllid with neem oil
Use reflective mulch to repel psyllids
Apply kaolin clay to deter insects
Maintain tree vigor with organic fertilizers
chemical_treatments: No cure - infected trees must be removed
prevention: Monitor regularly for Asian citrus psyllid

Use Bacillus subtilis as biological control
Remove infected twigs and branches
Prune for better air circulation

Spots enlarge rapidly and kill leaves

Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Improve drainage and air circulation
Use resistant varieties when available
chemical_treatments: Metalaxyl: Apply at first sign of disease

Monitor fields daily during humid weather
